title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 — Practice Test 2
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: What is 25 + 37 - 12?
How many triangles are in the figure below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 150' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='100,20 30,130 170,130' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='100' y1='20' x2='100' y2='130' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle with a vertical line from the top vertex to the base.
questions.solution: The line divides the large triangle into 2 small triangles, plus the original large triangle makes 3.

questions.question_text: Mia has 3 boxes. Each box holds 6 muffins. How many muffins does Mia have in total?
questions.solution: 3 boxes times 6 muffins each equals 18 muffins.

questions.question_text: Which number comes next in the pattern: 2, 5, 8, 11, ?
questions.solution: Each number increases by 3, so 11 + 3 = 14.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:15 PM and lasts 90 minutes. When does it end?
questions.solution: 90 minutes is 1 hour 30 minutes. 3:15 plus 1:30 equals 4:45 PM.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly 6 sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 80' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='20,40 40,15 60,40 40,65' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='40' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>A</text><polygon points='90,20 120,20 130,40 120,60 90,60 80,40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='105' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>B</text><polygon points='160,20 190,20 190,60 160,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='175' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>C</text><polygon points='220,60 240,20 260,60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='240' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>D</text><polygon points='280,20 295,35 290,55 270,55 265,35' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='280' y='78' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>E</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes labeled A through E: rhombus, hexagon, rectangle, triangle, pentagon.
questions.solution: A hexagon has 6 sides by definition.

questions.question_text: Tom, Ben, and Leo are standing in a line. Tom is not first. Leo is not last. Ben is in the middle. Who is first?
questions.solution: Ben is in the middle. Tom is not first so Tom is last. Therefore Leo is first.

questions.question_text: A ladybug has 6 legs. How many legs do 7 ladybugs have altogether?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 280 60' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='#e8e8e8'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='60' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='100' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='220' cy='30' r='12'/><circle cx='260' cy='30' r='12'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Seven small round ladybug shapes in a row.
questions.solution: 7 ladybugs times 6 legs each equals 42 legs.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 30 cents and an eraser costs 45 cents. Rosa buys 2 pencils and 1 eraser. How much does she pay?
questions.solution: 2 pencils cost 60 cents. Adding 45 cents gives 105 cents.

questions.question_text: In a jar there are red and blue marbles. There are 4 more red marbles than blue. If there are 7 blue marbles, how many marbles are in the jar?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 140 160' font-family='sans-serif'><path d='M30 40 Q30 20 70 20 Q110 20 110 40 L110 140 Q110 150 70 150 Q30 150 30 140 Z' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='95' text-anchor='middle' font-size='12'>jar of marbles</text></svg>
questions.solution: Red marbles = 7 + 4 = 11. Total = 11 + 7 = 18.

questions.question_text: A square is divided into 4 equal smaller squares. If the large square has side 8 cm, what is the perimeter of one small square?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 150 150' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='110' height='110' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='75' y1='20' x2='75' y2='130' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='75' x2='130' y2='75' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='145' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11'>8 cm</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A large square divided into 4 equal smaller squares by a horizontal and vertical line.
questions.solution: Each small square has side 4 cm. Perimeter = 4 x 4 = 16 cm.

questions.question_text: Noah has twice as many stickers as his sister Ava. Together they have 27 stickers. How many does Ava have?
questions.solution: If Ava has x stickers, Noah has 2x. Total 3x = 27, so x = 9.

questions.question_text: Lena writes the numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 on five cards. She wants to pick two cards whose sum is 7. How many different pairs can she pick?
questions.solution: Pairs that sum to 7 are (2,5) and (3,4). That is 2 pairs.
Subtracting four 7s is subtracting 28. 100 - 28 = 72.

questions.question_text: A train has 5 cars. Each car has 8 rows, and each row has 4 seats. How many seats are on the train?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 70' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><rect x='68' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><rect x='126' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><rect x='184' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><rect x='242' y='20' width='50' height='30'/></g><circle cx='25' cy='55' r='4' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/><circle cx='45' cy='55' r='4' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A simple train of 5 connected rectangular cars.
questions.solution: 5 cars x 8 rows x 4 seats = 160 seats.

questions.question_text: Which piece completes the rectangle below?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 180' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='20,20 120,20 120,60 80,60 80,100 20,100' fill='#e8e8e8' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='70' y='130' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11'>shape</text><rect x='160' y='20' width='30' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='175' y='65' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>A</text><rect x='200' y='20' width='40' height='30' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='220' y='65' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>B</text><polygon points='160,90 200,90 200,130 180,130 180,150 160,150' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='180' y='165' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>C</text><rect x='220' y='90' width='40' height='60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='240' y='165' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>D</text><polygon points='275,90 295,90 295,150 275,150' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><text x='285' y='165' text-anchor='middle' font-size='10'>E</text></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shaped piece and five candidate pieces labeled A through E.
questions.solution: The L-shape needs a 40x60 rectangle to complete it into a full rectangle, matching piece D.

questions.question_text: Five friends share 40 candies. Ivy gets 10. The rest is shared equally among the other four. How many candies does each of them get?
questions.solution: 40 - 10 = 30 candies left for 4 friends. 30 / 4 = 7.5.

questions.question_text: Four cats sit in a row. Whiskers is next to Mittens. Shadow is at an end. Boots is not next to Shadow. In which position is Mittens counting from Shadow?
questions.solution: Shadow is at one end. Boots cannot be next to Shadow, so position 2 is Whiskers or Mittens. Since Whiskers is next to Mittens, the order from Shadow is Shadow, Whiskers, Mittens, Boots. Mittens is 3rd.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is 12 cm long and 5 cm wide. What is the length of its diagonal?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 200 120' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='150' height='70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='170' y2='90' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 4'/><text x='95' y='110' text-anchor='middle' font-size='11'>12 cm</text><text x='5' y='60' font-size='11'>5</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle 12 cm by 5 cm with a dashed diagonal drawn.
questions.solution: By the Pythagorean theorem, diagonal = sqrt(12^2 + 5^2) = sqrt(169) = 13 cm.

questions.question_text: What is the smallest whole number greater than 1 that divides 30, 42, and 60 with no remainder?
questions.solution: All three numbers are even, so 2 divides each. 2 is the smallest whole number greater than 1 that works.

questions.question_text: Sam thinks of a number. He doubles it, then adds 5, and gets 17. What was Sam's number?
questions.solution: Undoing the steps: 17 - 5 = 12, then 12 / 2 = 6.

questions.question_text: A gardener plants 4 rows of tulips. Each row has 1 more tulip than the previous. The first row has 5 tulips. How many tulips are planted in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 120' font-family='sans-serif'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' fill='#e8e8e8'><circle cx='15' cy='20' r='5'/><circle cx='35' cy='20' r='5'/><circle cx='55' cy='20' r='5'/><circle cx='75' cy='20' r='5'/><circle cx='95' cy='20' r='5'/><circle cx='15' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='35' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='55' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='75' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='95' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='115' cy='45' r='5'/><circle cx='15' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='35' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='55' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='75' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='95' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='115' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='135' cy='70' r='5'/><circle cx='15' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='35' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='55' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='75' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='95' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='115' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='135' cy='95' r='5'/><circle cx='155' cy='95' r='5'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Four rows of tulip circles with 5, 6, 7, and 8 tulips respectively.
questions.solution: Rows have 5, 6, 7, 8 tulips. Total = 5 + 6 + 7 + 8 = 26.

questions.question_text: What is the sum of all even numbers from 2 to 20?
A snail climbs 5 meters up a wall during the day but slips 2 meters down each night. The wall is 11 meters tall. On which day does the snail reach the top?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 150 220' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='50' y='20' width='50' height='180' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='100' y2='50' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><line x1='50' y1='110' x2='100' y2='110' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><line x1='50' y1='170' x2='100' y2='170' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><text x='110' y='25' font-size='10'>11 m</text><text x='20' y='195' font-size='14'>@</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A vertical 11 meter wall with markings and a snail at the bottom.
questions.solution: End of day 1: 5 m. End of day 2 morning climb from 3 m reaches 8 m. After night: 6 m. On day 3 snail climbs from 6 m to 11 m and reaches the top.
